pattern: Running prefix sums turn the count of subarrays hitting a target into a tally of how many earlier prefixes differ from the current one by exactly the goal.
hints: A subarray sum equals the difference of two prefix sums, so fix the right end and ask how many left ends work.
Keep a count of every prefix sum seen so far; for the current prefix p, the subarrays ending here with the goal number a prefix equal to p minus goal.
Walk left to right maintaining the running sum and a frequency map of prior sums, adding the count of (running minus goal) at each step, with the empty prefix seeded as sum zero.

edge_inputs.description: all zeros with goal zero counts every nonempty subarray
goal larger than total ones gives zero
goal zero counts only the zero-runs, not the ones
all ones, goal in the middle

stress.description: random binary array with a goal drawn within the achievable range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    total = sum(nums)
    goal = rng.randint(0, total) if total > 0 else 0
    return [nums, goal]
stress.note: goal capped at total ones so many matches exist, including zero-sum runs

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def numSubarraysWithSum(self, nums, goal):
        from collections import defaultdict
        seen = defaultdict(int)
        seen[0] = 1
        running = 0
        count = 0
        for x in nums:
            running += x
            count += seen[running - goal]
            seen[running] += 1
        return count
